code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test21-OCT-2015Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or another chronic kidney disease with poor glycemic control and possible dehydration. Recommend further nephrological and diabetic evaluation.
